revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Funabaru Kofun]][[ja:船原古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Fukuoka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Funabaru Kofun | native_name =船原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Funabaru Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Funabaru Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Funabaru Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.71472222222222|N|130.5025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Koga|qid=Q768356|1=Koga, Fukuoka}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|42|53|N|130|30|9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Funabaru Kofun'''|船原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Taniyama neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Koga, Fukuoka|en|Koga, Fukuoka|qid=Q768356|lt=Koga|1=Koga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245841|title=船原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Funabaru Kofun was discovered in 1995 during land reclamation for farmland improvement. It is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is estimated to have been constructed between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. The existing length is 37.4 meters, and the restored total length is estimated to be over 45 meters. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2012 to 2013. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a horizontal-entry stone chamber made of monoliths, with an anterior and posterior room. The site is noteworthy for its wealth of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, which were unusually buried in pits outside the main tumulus. The No. 1 earthen pit, which has an inverted L-shape, contained over 500 items. These included the first chandelier-shaped gilt-bronze {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|qid=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}} decorations found in Japan, including a cross-piece fitting with a green glass bead set in the center, and metal weapons, armor and agricultural tools. The luxury of the harness was comparable to the quality of the artifacts excavated from the [[:en:Fujinoki Kofun|Fujinoki Kofun]] in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. After the excavation was completed, both the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was backfilled. In April 2018, a plaza and parking lot were built adjacent to the tumulus, and it is possible to visit the exterior.<ref name="Bunka1"/>
The tumulus is approximately 4.7 kilometres southeast of {{ill|Koga Station (Fukuoka)|en|Koga Station (Fukuoka)|qid=Q5359651|lt=Koga Station|1=Koga Station (Fukuoka)}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} (12 minutes by car).
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
